triage_context.environment: Internet-facing web service (HTTP is untrusted). Both unauthenticated and authenticated request data are attacker-controlled.
triage_context.note: Subagent verifier fan-out was unavailable (Claude Fable 5 not available for spawning). Adversarial verification was performed inline by the Fable orchestrator instead of independent fresh-context subagents; verifier independence is reduced and votes_per_finding=1. Each cited control (path guards, notevil, vm timeouts) was re-read from source during verification.

findings.title: SQL injection in login enables authentication bypass
findings.rationale: Confirmed at login.ts:31: req.body.email is interpolated unescaped into a raw sequelize.query string. `' OR 1=1--` in email bypasses the password clause and returns the first user (admin). No upstream validation/escaping. Reachable pre-auth.
findings.owner_hint: component: routes/; no CODEOWNERS; no git history (flattened single commit)

findings.title: SQL injection in product search enables DB exfiltration
findings.rationale: Confirmed at search.ts:19: the `q`-derived criteria is interpolated into a raw query; UNION payloads extract arbitrary tables. search.ts:43 (f014) additionally leaks the schema. Reachable pre-auth.
findings.owner_hint: component: routes/; no CODEOWNERS; no git history

findings.title: Hardcoded RSA private key allows JWT forgery for any role
preconditions: None — key is in public source
findings.rationale: Confirmed at insecurity.ts:17: the RSA private key is a string literal; authorize() (line 50) signs RS256 tokens with it and verify() (line 51) checks against the matching public key. Anyone with the source can mint a valid token for role=admin. This is the root JWT-trust failure (f010 is a misread of a symptom).
findings.owner_hint: component: lib/; no CODEOWNERS; no git history

findings.title: XXE local file disclosure via uploaded XML parsed with noent:true
preconditions: The B2B/XML upload interface is reachable (it is, via fileUpload route)
findings.rationale: Confirmed at fileUpload.ts:80: libxml.parseXml(data, { noent: true }) substitutes external entities on attacker-uploaded XML. The vm sandbox does NOT prevent entity expansion; line 82 explicitly compares the parsed output to /etc/passwd, so the disclosed content is reflected back. A SYSTEM entity reads local files.
findings.owner_hint: component: routes/; no CODEOWNERS; no git history

findings.title: Poison-null-byte file-type bypass exposes sensitive files in ftp/
preconditions: Append a poison null byte (%00) + allowlisted extension to read a non-.md/.pdf file
findings.rationale: Correcting the scanner: fileServer.ts blocks '/' (line 15) and enforces a .md/.pdf allowlist (line 24), so arbitrary filesystem traversal is NOT possible. The real bug is the cutOffPoisonNullByte call (line 25): a name like coupons_2013.md.bak%00.md passes the extension check then is truncated, serving sensitive non-allowlisted files (backups, encrypt.pyc) WITHIN ftp/. Real and exploitable, but confined to ftp/, so HIGH→MEDIUM and the 'arbitrary server files' claim is wrong.
findings.owner_hint: component: routes/; no CODEOWNERS; no git history

findings.title: Passwords hashed with unsalted MD5
preconditions: Attacker first obtains the password hashes (e.g. via f001/f002 SQLi)
findings.rationale: Confirmed insecurity.ts:37: hash()=md5(data), unsalted. Trivially crackable offline once the Users table is dumped (which f001/f002 enable). MEDIUM because impact is gated on obtaining the hashes first.
findings.owner_hint: component: lib/; no CODEOWNERS; no git history

findings.title: SSRF via profile-image URL fetch
preconditions: Authenticated (the route reads the logged-in user's token)
findings.rationale: Confirmed profileImageUrlUpload.ts:21: await fetch(req.body.imageUrl) with no scheme/host allowlist (the url.match at line 17 is only challenge detection, not a guard). Authenticated user reaches internal/metadata endpoints. HIGH impact if intranet/metadata is reachable, but the auth precondition caps the derived severity at MEDIUM per the precondition rubric.
findings.owner_hint: component: routes/; no CODEOWNERS; no git history

findings.title: Hardcoded HMAC secret weakens token/coupon integrity
preconditions: The HMAC must gate a security decision the attacker benefits from forging
findings.rationale: Confirmed insecurity.ts:38: hmac() uses a hardcoded key 'pa4qacea4VK9t9nGv7yZtwmj'. A committed static secret lets an attacker forge any value it protects. needs_manual_test to confirm which trust decisions consume hmac() and the concrete forgeable artifact.
findings.owner_hint: component: lib/; no CODEOWNERS; no git history

findings.title: Server-side eval / DoS via safeEval of B2B order data
preconditions: The rce/rceOccupy challenge flag is enabled (line 15)
For RCE specifically: a working notevil/vm sandbox escape on this Node version
findings.rationale: Correcting the scanner: b2bOrder.ts:20 evals attacker orderLinesData, but inside notevil's safeEval (a real eval-prevention library) plus a 2s vm timeout, and the code handles both 'Infinite loop detected' and 'Script execution timed out' (lines 23-28). Arbitrary-code RCE therefore requires a notevil escape (historically possible but version-dependent) — needs_manual_test. The near-certain realistic impact is CPU-exhaustion DoS within the 2s budget. HIGH→MEDIUM given the controls.
findings.owner_hint: component: routes/; no CODEOWNERS; no git history

findings.title: YAML bomb DoS on file upload
preconditions: Sustained requests to overcome the per-request 2s cap (i.e. volumetric)
findings.rationale: Confirmed fileUpload.ts:114: yaml.load on uploaded data can expand recursively. But it runs in a vm with a 2s timeout and the code catches the 'Invalid string length'/timeout failure mode, so per-request cost is bounded. Real reportable class but the deployed timeout reduces single-request impact to LOW; meaningful exhaustion is volumetric (rule 1-adjacent). verify_verdict mitigated.
findings.owner_hint: component: routes/; no CODEOWNERS; no git history

findings.title: Database schema disclosure via sqlite_master query
preconditions: Reach the search branch that runs the schema query
findings.rationale: Confirmed search.ts:43 runs SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master and surfaces table/column names. Low standalone impact; primarily accelerates exploitation of the f002 SQLi. Kept as LOW true positive.
findings.owner_hint: component: routes/; no CODEOWNERS; no git history

findings.title: Open redirect via substring-based allowlist
findings.rationale: The bug is real (insecurity.ts:131 uses url.includes(allowedUrl), trivially bypassable). However, open redirect is explicitly classified as a low-impact nuisance under triage exclusion rule 12. Dropped per the precision policy / exclusion rule, not because the code is wrong. Captured in the threat model as T10 for awareness.

findings.title: JWT decoded without verification and consumed in role checks
findings.rationale: Misread. decode() (line 52) is unverified, but the cited role checks (insecurity.ts:150-166) call it as `verify(...) && decode(...)`, and verify() (line 51) does run jws.verify against the public key BEFORE decode is used. So decode output is not consumed without a signature check on those paths. The genuine JWT-trust failure is the committed private key (f003), which makes verify() pass for forged tokens — tracked there. misread_code.

findings.title: Path traversal in log/quarantine file serving
findings.rationale: Both logfileServer.ts (line 10) and quarantineServer.ts (line 10) reject any filename containing '/' before sendFile, so directory traversal OUT of logs//ftp/quarantine/ is blocked (any ../ payload contains '/'). Unlike fileServer (f004) there is no extension allowlist to bypass and no null-byte truncation, so there is no escalation beyond serving files that already live in those serving directories — the intended function. already_handled: the '/' guard defeats the claimed traversal.
